title: Henry M. Jackson Visitor Center Facts for Kids
content: The information desk inside the new Henry M. Jackson Visitor Center** is a special building in the Paradise area of Mount Rainier National Park. It's a place where visitors can learn about the park, get help, and find things they need for their trip. The first visitor center opened in 1966. It was originally called the Paradise Visitor Center. A brand new visitor center was finished in 2008, and the old building was taken down. Jackson Visitor Center at Rainier Paradise. The original Jackson Visitor Center, used from 1966 to 2008. Mount Rainier National Park was chosen to try out a special program called "Mission 66." This program aimed to improve services for visitors in national parks across the country. The idea for the Paradise Visitor Center was to create a place for day visitors. The first visitor center was designed by a team of architects from Honolulu and Tacoma, Washington. The new Jackson Visitor Center with the Tatoosh Range in the background.
